sections.title: PURPOSE AND SCOPE
sections.llm_prompt: Write Purpose and Scope for a quality risk assessment on {process_type} of {product_name}. State the objective (e.g., identify, evaluate, and mitigate risks to product quality and patient safety), the boundaries of the assessment, and patient impact. Reference ICH Q9(R1). {description}

sections.title: RISK QUESTION
sections.llm_prompt: State the risk question (or questions) being evaluated in this assessment for {process_type}. Example: 'What are the potential failure modes during [process step], their effects on product quality and patient safety, and how can they be mitigated?'

sections.title: RISK ASSESSMENT METHODOLOGY
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the risk assessment methodology used. Choose the most appropriate tool from ICH Q9(R1): FMEA (Failure Modes and Effects Analysis), FMECA, FTA (Fault Tree Analysis), HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points), HAZOP (Hazard and Operability Analysis), PHA (Preliminary Hazard Analysis), or a hybrid approach. For the selected method, define: severity scale (1-5), probability/likelihood scale (1-5), detectability scale (1-5), RPN calculation formula, acceptance threshold (e.g., RPN > 100 requires mitigation), and risk prioritization rules.

rows.label: Severity 1 - Negligible
rows.value: No impact on product quality or patient

rows.label: Severity 2 - Minor
rows.value: Minor deviation, no product or patient impact

rows.label: Severity 5 - Critical
rows.value: Serious product failure, direct patient harm

rows.label: Detectability 5 - Low
rows.value: Unlikely to be detected before release

sections.title: RISK IDENTIFICATION AND ANALYSIS

columns.title: S x P x D = RPN

sections.llm_prompt: For each process step or hazard in {process_type} of {product_name}, identify: failure modes, causes, effects on product quality and patient, and initial risk scoring (Severity x Probability x Detectability = RPN). Also classify as Low/Medium/High. Be comprehensive - typical assessment has 20-40 entries.

sections.title: RISK CONTROL AND MITIGATION
sections.llm_prompt: For each High and Medium risk identified, list the mitigation action (engineering control, administrative control, procedural change, additional testing, training, monitoring), the action owner, due date, expected residual RPN after mitigation, and whether residual risk is acceptable.

sections.title: RISK ACCEPTANCE AND REVIEW
sections.llm_prompt: Describe the risk acceptance criteria and review process. State the RPN threshold for acceptance (e.g., all risks must be reduced to RPN < 50 or formally accepted by QA). Describe the review cadence (annually or upon process change), escalation process for unacceptable risks, and documentation for risk acceptance.

sections.title: CONCLUSIONS AND RECOMMENDATIONS
sections.llm_prompt: Write conclusions and recommendations from the risk assessment for {process_type}. Summarize total risks identified, risks mitigated, residual risk level, and any recommendations for follow-up actions, process changes, or additional monitoring.

sections.title: REFERENCES
sections.llm_prompt: Generate references for a quality risk assessment. Include: ICH Q9(R1), ICH Q10, FDA Guidance on Quality Risk Management, 21 CFR 211, and relevant company SOPs (quality risk management procedure, change control, CAPA).

metadata.regulatory_framework: ICH Q9(R1), ICH Q10, 21 CFR 211, EU GMP Annex 20
